---
title: "3.5 Methods: How to Write Them"
subtitle: "Part 1: Method Basics"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Methods define object behavior

In object-oriented programming, a class combines:

::: {.tight-list}
- instance variables: state
- constructors: initialization
- <span class="term">methods</span> (方法): behavior
:::

This first deck focuses on defining, calling, and classifying methods.

## Defining and Calling Methods

### A method definition lives inside the class

![Calling methods in different contexts](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-5-methods-how-to-write-them/FlowChartCallingMethods.png){fig-align="center" width="66%"}

A method call such as `p.print()` transfers control to the method body, runs its statements, then returns to the caller.

## Method Header

### Read the method signature

```java
public void print()
{
    System.out.println(name);
}
```

Key parts:

::: {.tight-list}
- `public`: other classes can call it
- `void`: it does not return a value
- `print`: method name
- `()`: parameter list, empty here
:::

## Code Task

### Add and call an instance method

```java
public class Person
{
    private String name;

    public Person(String initialName)
    {
        name = initialName;
    }

    public void print()
    {
        System.out.println(name);
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        Person p = new Person("Ada");
        p.print();
    }
}
```

Expected output: `Ada`.

## `void` Methods

### A `void` method performs an action

Common `void` method jobs:

::: {.tight-list}
- print information
- update object state
- move an object
- draw something
:::

`void` methods do not return a value to the caller.

```java
public void celebrateBirthday()
{
    age++;
}
```

## Non-`void` Methods

### A non-`void` method returns one value

```java
public int getAge()
{
    return age;
}
```

Rules:

::: {.tight-list}
- the return type is written before the method name
- the method must return a compatible value
- code after a guaranteed `return` path does not run
:::

## Quick Check

### Classify the method

```java
public boolean isAdult()
{
    return age >= 18;
}
```

This method is:

::: {.tight-list}
- non-`void`
- return type `boolean`
- no parameters
- computes a value from instance data
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- identify a method header and method body
- distinguish a method definition from a method call
- explain what `void` means
- explain when a `return` statement is required
- trace control from a call to a method body and back
:::

## End

### Continue or return to the course outline

[Next: Getters and toString](3-5-part-2-getters-and-tostring.html)  
[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
